title: Bit Digital Inc (BTBT) Q1 2025 Earnings: Revenue Decline Amid Cloud Services Growth
text: Bit Digital reported challenging results for Q1 2025, with total revenue falling 17% year-over-year to $25.1 million. The company's bitcoin mining operations experienced particularly steep declines, with Bitcoin Mining Revenue at $7.8 million, down 64% year over year. The company's profitability metrics deteriorated significantly with Adjusted EBITDA swinging to negative $44.5 million from positive $58.5 million in the prior-year period, largely driven by a $49.2 million mark-to-market loss on digital asset holdings. The firm reported a net loss of $0.32 per fully diluted share. Despite overall revenue weakness, gross margin improved to 49% from 47%, as cloud services showed stronger performance with Cloud Services Revenue at $14.8 million, up 84% year over year. The company maintained a solid balance sheet with $57.6 million in cash and equivalents, plus approximately $80 million in digital assets. Total shareholder equity reached $417 million on $485 million in assets. Management indicated active pursuit of large contracts, with each potentially delivering an annualized revenue above $100 million, alongside investments in proprietary software platforms and a $65 million quarterly capital expenditure program.
